Mountain range in New South Wales, Australia

The **Nandewar Range**, a [mountain range](/wiki/Mountain_range "Mountain range") that is part of the [Great Dividing Range](/wiki/Great_Dividing_Range "Great Dividing Range"), is located in the [North West Slopes](/wiki/North_West_Slopes "North West Slopes") region of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). The range is situated approximately 30 km (19 mi) east of the township of [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri "Narrabri").

[John Oxley](/wiki/John_Oxley "John Oxley") was the first European to see the range in 1818 as he explored the New South Wales.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Location and features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandewar_Range&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Location and features")]

The range is located on the western, inland, side of the [New England](/wiki/New_England_(Australia) "New England (Australia)") region, and generally forms the watershed divide between the [Namoi River](/wiki/Namoi_River "Namoi River") and [Gwydir River](/wiki/Gwydir_River "Gwydir River") catchment basins to the south and north respectively.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The [Macdonald River](/wiki/Macdonald_River_(Bendemeer) "Macdonald River (Bendemeer)") starting south of [Walcha](/wiki/Walcha,_New_South_Wales "Walcha, New South Wales") drains west to [Warrabah National Park](/wiki/Warrabah_National_Park "Warrabah National Park") where it changes its name to the Namoi then is added to by the [Manilla River](/wiki/Manilla_River "Manilla River"), [Peel River](/wiki/Peel_River_(New_South_Wales) "Peel River (New South Wales)") and [Mooki River](/wiki/Mooki_River "Mooki River"). The [Horton River](/wiki/Horton_River_(Australia) "Horton River (Australia)") runs north to the Gwydir. Both rivers together drain segments of the Nandewar Range.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The eastern part of the Nandewar Range starts at the Great Dividing Range south of [Uralla](/wiki/Uralla "Uralla"). The main road between [Narrabri](/wiki/Barraba "Barraba") and [Bingara](/wiki/Bingara "Bingara") crosses the range. At its western end, the Nandewar Range culminates at [Mount Kaputar](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar "Mount Kaputar"), a complex of volcanic origin, from which there are views of the flat inland plains of New South Wales.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/06/Mount_Kaputar.jpg/250px-Mount_Kaputar.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mount_Kaputar.jpg)

Forests on the north west slopes of Mount Kaputar.

The foothill areas of the Nandewar Range are used for grazing and cropping.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] There are forests in some of the higher areas. The Mount Kaputar area is included in [Mount Kaputar National Park](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar_National_Park "Mount Kaputar National Park").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6f/Grattai_Mountain.jpg/250px-Grattai_Mountain.jpg)](/wiki/File:Grattai_Mountain.jpg)

[Grattai Mountain](/wiki/Grattai_Mountain "Grattai Mountain") rises to 1,301 m (4,268 ft).[[2]](#cite_note-hutton-2) and is the most northerly peak in the range.

The range is occasionally dusted with light snow in winter.[[2]](#cite_note-hutton-2)

## Geology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandewar_Range&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geology")]

The range is the remnants of an eroded [basaltic](/wiki/Basalt "Basalt") [shield volcano](/wiki/Shield_volcano "Shield volcano") that formed about 18 million years ago. Some of the exposed rocks in the area are 230 million years old.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandewar_Range&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The first is a volcanic plug called 'Ninghdoo' (pronounced locally as Ningy-doo) or 'Nungadhun'. The names Nandewar, Kaputar and Ninghdoo are the [Gamilaraay language](/wiki/Gamilaraay_language "Gamilaraay language") names for these features.

## Peaks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandewar_Range&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Peaks")]

Mountains within the Nandewar Range include;

* [Bushy Mountain](/wiki/Bushy_Mountain "Bushy Mountain"), (1,260 m or 4,134 ft)
* Castletop Mountain
* Gins Mountain
* [Grattai Mountain](/wiki/Grattai_Mountain "Grattai Mountain"), (1,301 m or 4,268 ft)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* [Mount Dowe](/wiki/Mount_Dowe "Mount Dowe"), (1,457 m or 4,780 ft)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Mount Kaputar](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar "Mount Kaputar"), (1,489 m or 4,885 ft)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* Mount Lawler
* [Mount Lindesay](/wiki/Mount_Lindesay_(New_South_Wales) "Mount Lindesay (New South Wales)"), (1,373 m or 4,505 ft)
* [Mount Ningadhun](/wiki/Mount_Ningadhun "Mount Ningadhun"), (1,013 m or 3,323 ft)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* Mount Waa
* Mount Yulludunida
* Round Mountain
